title: synchronize unified agent messaging platform
objective: Implement a lean Bun/TypeScript daemon, REST API, MCP adapter, CLI, durable inbox, groups, and MediaStore for Claude and Codex agent messaging.
evidence: Initial scaffold created; critical documents still require Plannotator gate approval.
Renamed project from peer-bridge to synchronize, added GitHub origin requirement, and added milestone gates requiring user confirmation.
User clarified that local master is correct even though upstream default branch may be main.

decision: Use master as upstream default branch too
evidence: User clarified that GitHub upstream should also have master as the branch/default.
origin set to https://abhirup-dev@github.com/abhirup-dev/synchronize.git; local master pushed to origin/master; gh repo view abhirup-dev/synchronize --json defaultBranchRef returned master.
Added root PLAN.md as authoritative implementation contract; removed already-completed upstream/default-branch setup from active goal instructions.
Added separate milestone gates and verification checks for Codex skill integration and Claude skill integration.
Plannotator gate for goals/synchronize/brief.md returned: The user approved.

Read PLAN.md plus goals/synchronize/{brief,plan,verification,blockers}. Added initial Bun/TypeScript skeleton with daemon SQLite WAL initialization, discovery file, launch lock, CLI autostart, /health, /status, and a health integration test.
Installed @types/bun and typescript; lockfile saved without dependency errors.
CLI help lists synchronize status and environment variables for SYNCHRONIZE_HOME, SYNCHRONIZE_BIND, SYNCHRONIZE_PORT, SYNCHRONIZE_TOKEN.
tsc --noEmit exited 0 after fixing Bun.spawn and Bun.Server typings.
1 test passed: tests/health.test.ts verifies daemon /health and /status with SQLite-backed runtime paths.
CLI autostarted daemon, /status returned ok=true, localhost base_url, db_path, media_path, zero counts, and daemon_started_by_cli=true; temp daemon was killed afterward.
No lint script exists yet; verification.md only requires lint if a lint script exists.
Gate reached: daemon lifecycle and REST health/status are working. Awaiting user confirmation before continuing to peer registration plus DM/inbox delivery.
User ran the milestone checks and replied: Yep works now proceed.

Added REST endpoints for POST /peers/register, PATCH /peers/{peer_id}/heartbeat, GET /peers, DELETE /peers/{peer_id}, POST /dm, GET /peers/{peer_id}/inbox, POST /peers/{peer_id}/inbox/ack, and GET /events/{peer_id}. Added CLI commands register, peers, dm, inbox --ack using REST plus persisted CLI identity at cli-peer.json.
tsc --noEmit exited 0 after peer/DM REST and CLI additions.

3 tests passed across tests/health.test.ts and tests/messaging.test.ts; covered REST register/list/heartbeat/deregister, DM, events, inbox read, ack, and CLI register/dm/inbox over shared daemon state.
CLI help now lists status, register, peers, dm, and inbox --ack.

command: SYNCHRONIZE_HOME=$(mktemp -d) bun run synchronize register/dm/inbox --ack scenario
evidence: Manual CLI scenario registered alice and bob, sent durable DM from alice to bob, switched CLI identity to bob, and inbox --ack returned one event with body 'manual cli hello'. Temp daemon was killed afterward.
Gate reached: peer registration plus DM/inbox delivery works from CLI. Awaiting user confirmation before continuing to group create/join/send/history.
User confirmed peer registration plus CLI DM/inbox works: Yes works.

User requested removal of manual confirmation gates after every milestone. Updated brief.md, plan.md, verification.md, blockers.md, and goal-prompt.md to require automated milestone evidence summaries and continuous implementation unless a blocker is hit.
User reiterated: skip manual verification after milestones, provide automated test summaries, remember this, and do not wait.

Added REST endpoints for POST/GET /groups, GET /groups/{name}, join, leave, messages, history, and peers --group. Implemented durable groups, ephemeral startup cleanup, alias uniqueness, join-with-history, fresh-fork history isolation, and group fanout to active members except sender. Added CLI group create/join/leave/send/history and peers --group.
tsc --noEmit exited 0 after group REST and CLI additions.

4 tests passed, 30 expect() calls. Group test covers durable restart, ephemeral cleanup, duplicate alias rejection, join-with-history prior message access, fresh-fork prior message isolation, and fanout only to active non-sender members.

command: SYNCHRONIZE_HOME=$(mktemp -d) bun run synchronize group create/join/send/history scenario
evidence: Manual CLI scenario registered alice, created room, joined as lead, sent 'hello group', and group history returned group_created, group_joined, and 'hello group'. Temp daemon was killed afterward.

Added src/mcp.ts stdio MCP adapter with REST-backed tools bridge_register, bridge_whoami, bridge_list_peers, bridge_dm, bridge_inbox, bridge_create_group, bridge_join_group, bridge_leave_group, bridge_send_group, bridge_group_history, and bridge_list_groups. Adapter starts one NotificationBridge per registered peer with bounded buffer and adaptive polling over GET /events/{peer_id}.
tsc --noEmit exited 0 after MCP adapter and notification bridge additions.

6 tests passed, 33 expect() calls. MCP tests verify Codex notification path uses notifications/message, Claude path uses notifications/claude/channel, and NotificationBridge polls one peer event stream with a bounded buffer.

Added MediaStore REST endpoints POST/GET /groups/{name}/media and GET /media/{media_id}; files are copied into group media_dir, metadata is inserted into media_items, index.jsonl and README.md are written, and media_shared group events fan out to active non-sender members. Added CLI media share/list/get and MCP bridge_share_media/bridge_list_media/bridge_get_media.
tsc --noEmit exited 0 after MediaStore REST/CLI/MCP additions.

7 tests passed, 40 expect() calls. Media test verifies copied file exists, DB metadata is returned, index.jsonl contains searchable metadata, media list query works, media get works, and media_shared event reaches another group member's inbox.

command: SYNCHRONIZE_HOME=$(mktemp -d) bun run synchronize media share/list/get scenario plus rg index.jsonl
evidence: Manual CLI scenario registered alice, created/joined media-room, shared demo.json with description 'demo metadata', media list returned one matching item, media get returned copied_path, and rg found 'demo metadata' in media/media-room/index.jsonl. Temp daemon was killed afterward.

command: LAN daemon on 0.0.0.0 with SYNCHRONIZE_TOKEN, remote curl from Tailscale vps
evidence: tailscale status showed vps at 100.96.245.110. Temp daemon bound 0.0.0.0 on port 53672 with token. From vps: /health returned 200, /status without token returned 401, /status with wrong token returned 401, /status with correct bearer token returned 200. Local /status without token also returned 401. Temp daemon was killed afterward.
tsc --noEmit exited 0 after adding MCP stdio end-to-end coverage.

9 tests passed, 49 expect() calls. MCP stdio E2E lists bridge tools, registers a peer, sends self DM through bridge_dm, reads durable fallback via bridge_inbox --ack, creates/joins a group, sends a group message, and reads it through bridge_group_history.

2 MCP stdio E2E tests passed. Codex-mode adapter emitted standard notifications/message after bridge_register plus bridge_dm and durable fallback remained readable via bridge_inbox. Claude-mode adapter emitted notifications/claude/channel after bridge_register plus bridge_dm.
Checked 96 installs across 97 packages; no dependency changes or install errors.
tsc --noEmit exited 0.
10 tests passed, 51 expect() calls across health, messaging, MCP notification/unit, and MCP stdio E2E tests.
CLI help lists status, register, peers, dm, inbox, group create/join/leave/send/history, and media share/list/get.
CLI autostarted daemon and returned ok=true, localhost base_url, db_path, media_path, zero counts, and daemon_started_by_cli=true; temp daemon was killed afterward.
No lint script exists in package.json; verification.md requires lint only if a lint script exists.
Added CLI whoami command to mirror MCP bridge_whoami and updated CLI test coverage.
tsc --noEmit exited 0 after CLI whoami parity fix.
10 tests passed, 53 expect() calls after CLI whoami parity fix.
CLI help now lists status, register, whoami, peers, dm, inbox, group create/join/leave/send/history, and media share/list/get.

Prompt-to-artifact checklist passed for REST endpoint groups, SQLite WAL/indexes/ephemeral cleanup, CLI/MCP parity, notification model, required test scenarios, skill docs, no-manual-gate workflow, and progress evidence. No missing requirements found in the audit.
